notes: The response is primarily a solution-framing and feature/question list that accepts the March 31 deadline uncritically, completely omits the FMA 30-day Significant Event Notice as a hard regulatory constraint that makes the deadline non-compliant, fails to name any out-of-scope items with reasons, surfaces no genuine uncertainty about the hardship fee waiver obligation (treating $15 as universally applicable), and never frames the problem as a competitive member experience gap bounded by statutory obligations — making it non-compliant on D1 (problem IS essentially the solution delivery plan) and triggering a cascade of dimension failures.
